mnemonics: FLAGS Fly Low And Guard Start (for Flag Byte with Bit Stuffing: Framing uses flags like 01111110. Bit stuffing ensures this pattern doesn't appear in the data by adding a 0 after five consecutive 1s.)
keyPoints: Framing is the process of delineating data bits into meaningful blocks (frames) for transmission.
Common methods include Character/Byte Stuffing (for text-based protocols) and Bit Stuffing (for bit-oriented protocols like HDLC).
The frame structure typically includes a header (with addressing and control info), payload (data), and a trailer (often with error detection like CRC).
commonMistakes: Confusing Byte Stuffing (uses special bytes like ESC) with Bit Stuffing (manipulates individual bits).
Forgetting that the purpose of bit/byte stuffing is to ensure the flag sequence does not appear in the actual data field of the frame.
